ablation_body: Each feature-removal policy was retrained and evaluated on the same held-out scenarios, and the full-feature reference policy was retrained at the same training budget so the deltas isolate the feature removal rather than training length. A positive delta means higher execution cost than the reference. This is an association inside the synthetic simulator, not a causal claim about real markets.
cap_classical: Classical schedule world · {n:,} common-random-number scenarios. All strategies share identical price, volume, and spread paths. Strategy IDs are evaluated in this world and differ from same-named rows in other tables.
cap_lob: Reactive LOB · in-distribution · {n:,} shared-scenario episodes. Strategy IDs are evaluated in this world; RL rows carry the training seed.
cap_rl: In-distribution RL comparison · {n:,} shared-scenario episodes (common random numbers across the stress matrix and regimes). RL rows carry the training seed.
cap_misspecification: Misspecified simulator · {n:,} shared-scenario episodes (paired with in-distribution on identical scenarios).
experiment_note: Each world (classical / reactive LOB / stress) is a separate evaluation with its own common-random-number scenario stream. Identically named base strategies in different tables are independent evaluations, and RL rows carry the training seed. Stress regimes also use common random numbers across regimes (paired).
val_selection_note: The best checkpoint is selected on the validation stream (disjoint from test); the metrics below use the independent test stream. The gap between the validation (selection) value and the independent test value reflects selection optimism. Multiple-comparison bias from picking the minimum across strategies is mitigated by the paired 95% CIs in the conclusion.
ac_body: Almgren–Chriss provides the transparent strategic benchmark. As risk aversion rises, the schedule becomes more front-loaded: faster trading pays more impact to reduce exposure to price movements. In the risk-neutral limit, the inventory path converges to TWAP.
chart.classical_cost: Classical strategy cost and tail risk
conclusion_template: {classical_stmt} {lob_stmt} {rl_statement} Rankings depend on the simulator and calibration; the significance here concerns Monte-Carlo (paired) error, not real-market causality.
data_note: All values come from locally generated synthetic data. Cost is positive when execution is worse than the arrival price. Intervals are 95% bootstrap intervals for mean cost.
executive_summary_body: Execution speed trades market impact against timing risk. Classical schedules make this trade-off explicit; passive orders add fill and adverse-selection risk; and a reactive book reveals costs that a no-footprint replay omits. Reinforcement learning is evaluated as a bounded tactical adjustment around an interpretable baseline and as a freer policy, using held-out common-random-number scenarios.
finding_reactive_template: The reactive TWAP cost was {reactive:.3f} bps versus {replay:.3f} bps in no-footprint replay, a difference of {delta:.3f} bps.
finding_best_significant: In {world}, {best} had the lowest mean cost, and its gap to the runner-up {runner} was significant under a paired (common-random-number) 95% bootstrap CI (Δ={delta:.3f} bps, CI[{lo:.3f}, {hi:.3f}]).
finding_best_not_significant: In {world}, {best} had the lowest mean, but its paired 95% CI versus the runner-up {runner} includes zero, so the difference is not significant (Δ={delta:.3f} bps, CI[{lo:.3f}, {hi:.3f}]).
finding_best_mean_only: In {world}, {best} had the lowest mean (runner-up {runner}, mean gap Δ={delta:.3f} bps). A paired (common-random-number) CI was unavailable, so significance was not assessed.
finding_rl_mean_only: In distribution, the best RL ({best}) had a lower mean cost than the best rule-based policy ({runner}) (mean gap Δ={delta:.3f} bps). A paired CI was unavailable, so significance is unassessed; the quick profile uses one seed.
finding_rl_significant: In distribution, the best RL ({best}) beat the best rule-based policy ({runner}) significantly (paired 95% CI, Δ={delta:.3f} bps, CI[{lo:.3f}, {hi:.3f}]). The quick profile uses one training seed, so seed robustness still needs separate checking.
finding_rl_not_significant: In distribution, the gap between the best RL ({best}) and the best rule-based policy ({runner}) has a paired 95% CI that includes zero, so it is not significant (Δ={delta:.3f} bps, CI[{lo:.3f}, {hi:.3f}]). Adaptivity alone does not guarantee lower economic cost.
finding_rl_unavailable: The in-distribution RL comparison was unavailable.
finding_shift_template: Under simulator misspecification, the best residual-RL run changed from {id_cost:.3f} bps in distribution to {shift_cost:.3f} bps, a shift of {delta:.3f} bps.
impact_body: Temporary impact is paid only while trading, permanent impact shifts subsequent prices, and transient impact decays with resilience. The decomposition is exact only because the simulator exposes its latent states. The square-root law is shown as a separate empirical diagnostic and is not added to the linear model.
limitations: Limitations and what this does not establish
limitations_body: This is a pedagogical synthetic market, not an exchange-grade simulator. It omits hidden liquidity, latency, venue fragmentation, strategic counterparties, manipulation controls, cross-impact, and calibration to real order-level data. Queue position and adverse selection are stylized. RL results depend on the simulator, reward design, training budget, and seed; the quick profile uses one training seed and cannot establish statistical superiority.
lob_body: Agent market orders consume displayed depth, walk deeper liquidity, widen the spread, and create permanent and transient footprint. Passive orders face a FIFO queue proxy and may be filled before adverse moves. The replay control shares exogenous random draws but deliberately removes the agent footprint.
market_setup_body: Inventory is the positive number of shares remaining. Child quantity is nonnegative and reduces inventory. For both sells and buys, positive implementation shortfall means worse execution than arrival. Prices, volume, spreads, and liquidity are generated locally from the recorded configuration and seed.
methodology_body: Strategies within each comparison share common random numbers. Training, validation, and test seed streams are disjoint. Economic implementation shortfall is reported separately from the shaped RL reward. Hard constraints prevent over-execution, cap child orders and participation, enforce a price collar, and force terminal liquidation. Confidence intervals quantify Monte Carlo uncertainty, not model uncertainty.
model_theory: Model mathematics and intuition: equations, variables, strengths and weaknesses
profile_note_template: Profile: {profile}; master seed: {seed}; horizon: {horizon:.0f} s; order: {inventory:,.0f} shares; arrival price: {price:.2f}.
reactive_lob: Market versus limit orders and reactive LOB
related_work: Related work: problem, proposal, results, and open questions
report_subtitle: A synthetic, reproducible visual laboratory for market impact, resilient liquidity, transaction-cost analysis, and safe RL execution
reproduction_body: From the project directory, run make install, make test, and make demo. The quick profile regenerates the data, checkpoints, figures, executed notebook, and both standalone reports without external market data or network access.
rl_body: Residual RL scales and times an Almgren–Chriss baseline through the same safety layer as every other policy. Free RL uses a TWAP-sized action grid. Results are reported on fixed held-out scenarios, stress regimes, and a misspecified simulator. Training reward is never presented as economic profit and loss.
rl_seed_warning: The quick profile trained one seed. Seed sensitivity is therefore unknown; the full profile is configured for three seeds.
strategy_comparison_body: The schedule-world comparison uses identical price, volume, and spread paths for every strategy. Mean cost must be read together with dispersion and CVaR: a low average can conceal terminal cleanup or tail exposure.
